facts.shortSummary: The party equips itself in Cleenseau, slips through the portal under concealment, and maps a cautious long route into the thorn maze beyond.
facts.longSummary: After recapping the queen's request that they permanently close the Feywild portal, the party gathers specialized supplies, prays for concealment, and enters the portal. On the far side they find a cold, soggy thorn maze under ritual strain, spot magical tendrils running toward a distant peak, and use Es*tiaslos and Izgil's mapping skills to choose the longer route inward on the theory that the direct path is a trap.

location.context: final preparations in town, the hidden approach to the portal, and the first mapped passages beyond it
location.notes: The beat begins on the mortal side and ends once the party has committed to a route through the maze.

npcs.name: Ethlenn
npcs.notes: Izgil prays to Ethlenn for help before the party enters the portal, but nothing visibly happens.

items.name: barrel of tar
items.notes: Viepuck brings the tar along as part of the plan to destroy the portal structure.

facts.shortSummary: Hostile grabby vines force the party to learn the maze's rules the hard way and fight their way onward with radiant magic and lantern light.
facts.longSummary: At the roofed portion of the maze the party discovers that the hanging thorn vines are very much alive and react badly to experimentation. Necrotic damage only enrages them, physical attacks are ineffective, and each dead vine bursts with poisonous ichor, so Celyn and Izgil settle on burning them down with radiant cantrips while Robin's lantern and magical lights keep the twisting passages navigable on the way to the center.

location.context: roofed passages and vine-choked arches on the long route toward the core
location.notes: This beat covers the first active defensive layer inside the maze.

items.name: Robin's lantern
items.notes: The lantern proves unusually helpful for choosing turns in the dark maze.

mainEnemies.name: grabby vines
mainEnemies.notes: Animated thorn vines guarding the route into the maze's covered center.

combat.notes: The party discovers the vines' damage resistances and settles on radiant magic as the reliable answer.

facts.shortSummary: At the maze's heart, the party survives a collapsing chamber, destroys the thorn knot powering the portal, kills its guardian, and escapes through the dead maze.
facts.longSummary: The center chamber reveals a massive thorn knot fed by ritual energy from a distant peak, and the room immediately turns hostile when the roof drops on Viepuck. Once he escapes, the party studies the knot under pressure, with Viepuck opening a Far Realms rift against it and Celyn forcing his insight through with divine aid while Izgil floods the chamber with moonlight. As the immobile knot burns down, a thorn monster emerges to protect it, but the party stalls and beats it long enough for Viepuck to destroy the last glowing remnant, collapsing the ritual, stilling the maze, and letting the group escape after setting more of the thorns ablaze.

location.context: the roofed core chamber and the passages leading out through the now-stilled maze
location.notes: The chamber holds both the ritual knot that sustains the portal and the guardian that tries to stop the party.

npcs.name: Greymalkin
npcs.notes: Greymalkin stays close during the collapse and the final chamber fight.

items.name: barrel of tar
items.notes: The tar barrel is lost under the collapse, then later ignited to help spread more fire through the maze.

mainEnemies.name: thorn knot
mainEnemies.notes: The immobile ritual focus sustaining the portal.

mainEnemies.name: thorn monster
mainEnemies.notes: A guardian that emerges from the hedge to break Izgil's concentration and protect the knot.

combat.notes: The source frames this as a literal Combat With The Gazebo, with the knot as the objective and the thorn monster as its active defender.

facts.shortSummary: After sealing the portal, the party races east through the Gloomshaper's lands, finds uncannily favorable travel, and reaches a suspicious cottage in a pine forest.
facts.longSummary: Once the maze is behind them, the party cloaks its tracks again and flees east in hopes of reaching the edge of the Gloomshaper's directly claimed territory before resting. The escape goes unnervingly well, as if they have stepped onto a cosmic conveyor belt, and after two hard hours they reach a gentler stretch of hills and pine woods where a welcoming path leads straight to a cottage watched by a raven with one bright human eye.

location.to: pine forest cottage in the Feywild
location.context: a hurried eastward retreat across the Gloomshaper's territory into a less oppressive pine wood
location.notes: The beat ends with the first arrival at the cottage.

npcs.name: Gloomshaper
npcs.context: archfey whose territory the party is fleeing
npcs.notes: The group explicitly plans to get beyond the Gloomshaper's directly claimed lands before resting.

npcs.name: Greymalkin
npcs.notes: Greymalkin helps judge the uncanny travel and the approach to the cottage.

npcs.name: old lady of the pine cottage
npcs.notes: She receives the party at the cottage and immediately enforces her own rules of hospitality.

facts.shortSummary: Inside the cottage, the party survives a strict etiquette test, dreadful tea, and Greymalkin's outrage while slowly learning that a goat-headed servant fled Izar the Red.
facts.longSummary: The cottage proves to belong to an archetypal little old lady who insists on civilized behavior, even as the party discovers a goat-headed servant in the back and Greymalkin loudly objects to the whole arrangement. By sitting, speaking politely, repairing broken crockery, and flattering her truly terrible tea, the group gradually restores her approval. In the course of that careful social dance, Viepuck learns that the goatboy once served Izar the Red, a frightening figure who keeps trophies taken from defeated fey.

location.primary: pine forest cottage in the Feywild
location.context: the front room, tea table, and immediate grounds around the cottage
location.notes: Most of the beat turns on the old lady's hospitality rules and the party's efforts not to offend her further.

npcs.name: old lady of the pine cottage
npcs.notes: She is benign enough, but only if the party follows her standards of etiquette.

npcs.name: Greymalkin
npcs.notes: Greymalkin's agitation over the cottage keeps Robin from sitting at first and nearly derails the visit.

npcs.name: goatboy servant
npcs.context: former servant of Izar the Red
npcs.notes: The party finds him in the back of the cottage, partially transformed and eating hay.

npcs.name: Izar the Red
npcs.context: frightening master named by the goatboy's memories
npcs.notes: Viepuck learns that Izar collects trophies from defeated fey, including a unicorn horn.

npcs.name: Cloudspinner
npcs.notes: The old lady asks whether the party has come from Amberglow after noticing Robin's lantern.

items.name: Robin's lantern
items.notes: The lantern's origin in Amberglow is what prompts the old lady's question about the party's travels.

facts.shortSummary: Questioning the goatboy reveals that Izar keeps wings that may be Greymalkin's, and over dinner the old lady all but invites the party to move against him.
facts.longSummary: With the old lady's permission, the party speaks to the goatboy directly and learns that his brother is still trapped in Izar's service and that Izar keeps a pair of wings in his trophy room that may once have belonged to Greymalkin. Dinner follows, with the old lady serving a deeply strange but carefully endured meal while the party discusses Izar more openly. She makes it clear that he is bad for the neighborhood and that she would not object if someone did something about him.

location.primary: pine forest cottage in the Feywild
location.context: the back room where the goatboy is kept and the dinner table inside the cottage
location.notes: The beat shifts from quiet questioning to a more explicit anti-Izar conversation over dinner.

npcs.name: old lady of the pine cottage
npcs.notes: She sends the party to speak with the goatboy and later openly says the neighborhood would be better off without Izar.

npcs.name: goatboy servant
npcs.context: informant asking help for his brother
npcs.notes: He confirms that Izar has wings in his trophy room and asks the party to help his brother still trapped in the keep.

npcs.name: Greymalkin
npcs.context: possible original owner of the stolen wings
npcs.notes: The revelation about the wings lands as a personal stake in the next move against Izar.

npcs.name: Izar the Red
npcs.notes: The party learns more clearly that Izar's trophy room may hold both Greymalkin's wings and other trophies taken from defeated fey.

items.name: Greymalkin's wings
items.notes: The goatboy says a set of wings in Izar's trophy room may belong to Greymalkin.

facts.shortSummary: By morning the party has traded for the goatboy, taken directions to Izar's keep, and marched halfway there before stopping at lunch to decide how they can help him.
facts.longSummary: In the morning the old lady gives the party directions, and Viepuck trades a hundred weirdberries for the goatboy, whose transformation has already shifted again. She makes clear that she will not reverse the curse herself, but the goatboy may still be useful if he can guide the party into Izar's keep. The group heads out, pauses halfway there over lunch, and closes the session while deciding what limited aid they can offer him before the confrontation ahead.

location.from: pine forest cottage in the Feywild
location.context: the morning departure from the cottage and the midday halt halfway to the keep
location.notes: The source ends before the party reaches Izar's stronghold.

npcs.name: old lady of the pine cottage
npcs.notes: She provides directions and agrees to part with the goatboy in exchange for weirdberries.

npcs.name: goatboy servant
npcs.notes: The party takes him along in hopes that he can help them reach or infiltrate Izar's keep.

npcs.name: Izar the Red
npcs.notes: The party sets out toward Izar's place as the next concrete objective.

items.name: weirdberry cakes
items.notes: The party still has the cakes from dinner but decides it is too early to use them.
